description: Body for catalog-relationships-create. Always lands in PROPOSED status until reviewed.

confidence.description: Agent's confidence (0..1) that this relationship is correct. Drives the review queue — low-confidence edges surface for human approval before agents trust them for joins.

discovered_in_run_id.description: ID of the CatalogTraversalRun this relationship was discovered in. Leave null for ad-hoc proposals.

generator_model.description: Model that proposed the relationship — same convention as on nodes and columns.

kind.description:
Relationship type. `foreign_key` when the source column references a target PK. `same_entity` when two columns identify the same business object (Stripe.customer_id ≈ Postgres.users.id). `lineage` when the target table is derived from the source. `declared_join` for an officially supported join. `join_candidate` for an inferred-but-unconfirmed join.

* `foreign_key` - foreign_key
* `same_entity` - same_entity
* `lineage` - lineage
* `declared_join` - declared_join
* `join_candidate` - join_candidate

reasoning.description: Free-text justification for the proposal — the data points or column-name signals the agent used. Surfaces in the review UI so a human can decide whether to accept or reject.

source_column_id.description: Narrows the source side to a specific column. Set for foreign-key and join edges; null for table-level lineage.

source_node_id.description: ID of the node the relationship originates from — e.g. the fact table, source side of a join.

target_column_id.description: Narrows the target side to a specific column. Same semantics as source_column_id.

target_node_id.description: ID of the node the relationship points to. For joins this is the other table; for foreign keys, the referenced table.
